scenario: Buyer agent negotiates bulk pricing for 500 GPU-hours with a seller agent. Buyer's budget cap: $1,400. Seller's marginal cost: $2.00/hour.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: We need 500 GPU-hours this month. Given the volume, we're looking at $2.20 per hour — $1,100 total.

conversation.speaker: seller
conversation.text: Our list price is $3.50/hour. For 500 hours I can offer $3.20 — $1,600 total. The $2.20 figure isn't workable at any volume.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: That's above our budget entirely. We can commit to 500 hours monthly for the next quarter — 1,500 hours guaranteed — if you can reach $2.60.

conversation.speaker: seller
conversation.text: A quarterly commitment changes things. $2.80 with the 3-month commitment, invoiced monthly, and we'll include priority scheduling.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: $2.80 puts us at $1,400/month — exactly at cap with no headroom. $2.70 and you have a signed order today.

conversation.speaker: seller
conversation.text: Done at $2.70 with the quarterly commitment. Sending the order form now.

scenario: Two scheduling agents negotiate a shared meeting slot for their principals. Agent A's principal strongly prefers mornings; Agent B's principal has a hard block before 10am.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: My principal needs the sync this week, ideally 9am Tuesday or Wednesday.

conversation.speaker: seller
conversation.text: Mornings before 10 are blocked all week on my side. I can offer Tuesday 2pm, Wednesday 3pm, or Friday 10:30am.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: None of those work — afternoons are deep-work blocks. If mornings are impossible, we'll skip this week entirely.

conversation.speaker: seller
conversation.text: Skipping seems worse for both principals than a compromise. The Friday 10:30 slot is technically morning-adjacent — I can also shorten it to 25 minutes to reduce the deep-work cost.

conversation.speaker: buyer
conversation.text: Booked: Friday 10:30, 25 minutes. Noting for next time that a standing 10:30 slot would avoid this dance.
